description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a military-style camouflage paint scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically. The fuselage is olive green with tan and brown accents, and the nose is adorned with a painted shark mouth and eyes, giving it a distinctive, aggressive appearance. The propeller is wooden and mounted at the front, while a single open cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, with a visible machine gun mounted in front of the pilot's seat. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in matching camouflage colors. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft, likely inspired by World War I-era planes.
description: A vintage, camouflage-painted biplane with a wooden propeller, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, featuring a shark mouth nose art and military markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked biplane wings, open cockpit, and shark mouth nose art.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin wings, tail section, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible cockpit, machine gun, and camouflage pattern.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the wooden propeller, shark mouth nose, and dual wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with no canopy.
